data.description: Epic for adding per-harmonic fine control (gain, stereo pan, phase) for the cymatic laser mirror experiment. Architecture: standalone harmonic_shaper component using direct Python sine synthesis (sounddevice) for phase accuracy. Controlled via Minilab3 MIDI, a web UI, and an automation/experiment API. Feeds a dataset logger for cymatic research / intuition model training.
data.title: EPIC: Cymatic Harmonic Shaper

data.description: New Python package harmonic_shaper/. Listens to /beacon/voice/on|off|freq OSC broadcasts. Maintains VoiceParameterStore: dict by harmonic_n → {gain, pan, phase}. Real-time audio synthesis via sounddevice (pure sines, stereo). Applies parameters per-sine on each buffer callback. Thread-safe: audio callback reads atomically, control surfaces write. Includes watchdog for audio device disconnect/reconnect and panic/reset path. Acceptance: 5 harmonics playing simultaneously with independently controllable amplitude.
data.title: Card 1: harmonic_shaper Core Engine

data.description: Spike to define and validate timing constraints before building the full shaper. Deliverables: jitter budget spec, clock sync strategy (monotonic_ns for logger, audio frame counter for sync), phase calibration loopback procedure (measure actual vs commanded phase), and decision on whether sounddevice callback precision is sufficient. Informs Card 1 implementation.
data.title: Card 0b: Timing Architecture & Phase Calibration (spike)

data.description: Expand the existing secondary MIDI handler (currently only f1 mod) to control shaper params. Knobs 1-5: gain for active harmonic slots. Page button switches to pan row (knobs 1-5) or phase row (knobs 1-5). Soft-takeover/pickup logic for knobs. Bidirectional sync: MIDI → VoiceParameterStore, UI reflects state. Minilab3 is already wired via SECONDARY_MIDI_PORT_PATTERN='Minilab'.
data.title: Card 2: Minilab3 MIDI Shaper Mapping

data.description: Lightweight local web server (FastAPI + static HTML/JS). Shows active harmonic channels as a mini mixer: gain fader, pan slider, phase knob per harmonic_n. WebSocket push for real-time sync. UI changes sent to VoiceParameterStore. Hardware (Minilab3) changes reflected in UI. Should not be in the hard real-time path.
data.title: Card 3: Shaper Control Web UI

data.description: OSC receiver: /shaper/harmonic/<n>/gain, /shaper/harmonic/<n>/pan, /shaper/harmonic/<n>/phase. REST API: PUT /api/harmonic/{n}/params {gain, pan, phase}, GET /api/state, POST /api/panic. All writes go through VoiceParameterStore. Agent/script-accessible. Acceptance: curl or Python script can drive shaper programmatically.
data.title: Card 4: OSC + HTTP Automation API

data.description: experiments/ directory with Python experiment scripts. API: Experiment.run() → time-indexed sequence of parameter states. Supports linear sweeps, oscillating sweeps, randomized walks, preset snapshots. Example: slowly increase harmonic 3 phase 0→360° over 10s while recording. Drives shaper via HTTP API or direct VoiceParameterStore. Acceptance: example experiment runs, produces audible change, logs data.
data.title: Card 5: Experiment Runner

data.description: Logs VoiceParameterStore snapshots at configurable interval (default 100ms). Per-row: session_id, experiment_id, timestamp (monotonic_ns), harmonic_n, gain, pan, phase. Session metadata: f1, harmonic_set, calibration_snapshot, environmental_notes, audio_device. Output: CSV + JSON. Future: video frame sync markers. Acceptance: after a run, a CSV exists with full timestamped parameter history.
data.title: Card 6: Dataset Logger

data.description: Notes triggered by Launchpad start playing in the Shaper but never stop. Even the panic button does not silence them. Need to debug the path from OSC /beacon/voice/off and the Panic action, down to the sounddevice audio callback to ensure voices are properly deactivated or muted.
data.title: Card 7: Debug sticky notes in Shaper component

data.description: 1. The UI displays f1=58.3Hz instead of the configured 60Hz. Need to trace how f1 is initialized and updated. \n2. The Minilab3 knobs are not intuitively mapped or not working. Need to map them to Gain, Pan, Phase correctly and provide user instructions.
data.title: Card 8: Fix f1 UI display and refine Minilab3 mappings

data.description:
1. Enforce max 5 voice polyphony. 
2. Leave the lowest harmonic as an untouched reference.
3. Hard-map 4 sliders -> Gain (upper 4 voices).
4. Hard-map 4 knobs -> Pan (upper 4 voices).
5. Hard-map 4 knobs -> Phase (upper 4 voices).
6. Remove Minilab Pages (Pad) logic to simplify the workflow.
data.title: Card 9: Dedicated Hardware Mapping for 5-Voice Polyphony
